title: Why Oliver Bearman was so much faster than Franco Colapinto when he crashed
summary:
Haas Formula 1 team principal Ayao Komatsu has absolved Franco Colapinto of any blame after Oliver Bearman crashed out of the Japanese Grand Prix.
After qualifying a lowly 18th and making an earlier pitstop than most, Bearman was battling Colapinto for 17th when he lost control at 306km/h coming into Spoon, crashing his Haas car with a 50G impact. The young Briton hobbled out of his car and was ...Keep reading

title: Monster 50G F1 crash sparks fury as Antonelli makes history
summary: Kimi Antonelli made history by winning the Japanese Grand Prix, making him the first teenager to win multiple F1 races and the youngest driver to lead the world championship. However, the big talking point surrounded a huge 50G crash for Haas' Oliver Bearman, triggered by a staggering closing speed on Alpine's Franco Colapinto. Thankfully, Bearman escaped unharmed but drivers after the race issued their fury at the accident even happening in the first place. Join RacingNews365's Ian Parkes and Nick Golding, as they dissect the Japanese GP!
